title: Scientists Help Mice with Sick Livers by Removing Zombie Cells
summary: Scientists at UCLA found special cells called zombie cells that build up in the liver and cause damage. These cells are like broken cars that block traffic and make tissues sick. In mice with fatty liver disease from eating unhealthy food, removing these zombie cells made the liver healthier and smaller. The scientists used a drug to target only the zombie cells, and the mice got better even without changing their diet. This research shows that cleaning out these bad cells can help reverse liver problems, which might help people with similar diseases in the future.

questions.question: What are zombie cells?
options: Cells that stop dividing but stay active
Cells that help the body fight germs
Cells that make the liver stronger
Cells that only appear in young animals
questions.correct_answer: Cells that stop dividing but stay active

background_read: The liver is an organ in the body that helps clean the blood and digest food. Sometimes, eating too much unhealthy food can cause fatty liver disease, where fat builds up and damages the liver. Scientists study this by using mice, which are similar to humans in some ways, to find new treatments. Zombie cells are old cells that don't die but cause problems by releasing harmful signals. Removing them might help people with liver diseases live healthier lives.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Removed zombie cells from mice livers to reverse damage
WHERE: In the liver of mice
WHY: To help treat fatty liver disease and improve health

perspectives.description: This research offers hope for new treatments by targeting zombie cells to heal livers without diet changes.
